summary: MSAL popup→redirect fix and mobile SocialAuthButtons verified. PRIMARY assertion PASSED: clicking [data-testid=auth-microsoft-btn] triggers a FULL-PAGE navigation to https://login.microsoftonline.com/organizations/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id=5de6c39a-bf40-4f42-88d8-f755a7797cc9&scope=openid%20email%20profile%20offline_access&redirect_uri=<origin>&response_mode=fragment&prompt=select_account&response_type=code&code_challenge_method=S256&state=<b64({interactionType:'redirect'})>&x-client-SKU=msal.js.browser&x-client-VER=5.17.0 — this is loginRedirect (not loginPopup), confirmed by the state payload literally containing 'interactionType':'redirect'. MsalRedirectHandler mounts globally in App.js inside BrowserRouter and silent-returns on Landing/ (with junk ?code=fake_code&state=fake_state param) — zero page errors. All 11 backend regressions pass in 2.14s (test_oauth_new.py). Email/password admin login (siddharth.bose@iemlabs.com) and fresh signup both reach /chat. Facebook button fires info toast. Google GSI iframe renders. Mobile: /app/mobile/src/components/SocialAuthButtons.js exists, exports default a React component, imported and rendered inside LoginScreen.js (line 43) and RegisterScreen.js (line 47); all three files parse cleanly with babel-preset-expo. expo-apple-authentication ^57.0.1 present in /app/mobile/package.json dependencies.
action_items: USER ACTION (unchanged from iter_3/5): Add https://iema-ai-platform.preview.emergentagent.com as an Authorized JavaScript Origin in Google Cloud Console; add it as a Redirect URI (SPA) in the Azure App Registration; and as a Return URL in Apple Services ID. For real MS OAuth completion the user (siddharth.bose@iemlabs.com) with 2FA must be tested manually — headless automation cannot complete the interactive 2FA step.
critical_code_review_comments: MsalRedirectHandler.jsx creates a NEW PublicClientApplication on every mount (not the cached module-level instance from AuthPage.jsx). If a user lands on /login after a Microsoft redirect, BOTH the global handler AND AuthPage's own MSAL init useEffect will call handleRedirectPromise() on separate MSAL instances — MSAL v3+ tolerates this but only one will observe the response. Currently AuthPage's handler is gated by sessionStorage.iema_msal_pending marker so it will still fire when the marker is set; the global one clears the marker first. Race is benign but could double-toast on error paths. Consider consolidating into one handler (either global-only or page-only).
MsalRedirectHandler.jsx L22: hash check uses window.location.hash but MSAL responds with response_mode=fragment — this is correct. Good.

MsalRedirectHandler.jsx L43: sessionStorage.removeItem in the catch is fine, but the toast at L46 will fire for ANY error, including errors unrelated to MSAL flow (e.g., if /auth/oauth-config fails). Consider guarding the toast behind a check that the error came from MSAL/handleRedirectPromise specifically.

Mobile SocialAuthButtons.js L11: WebBrowser.maybeCompleteAuthSession() is called at module scope — this is the standard pattern for expo-auth-session but note it must also be called in the root App if the OAuth completion happens after a cold-start deep link.

Mobile SocialAuthButtons.js L15-17: OAuth client IDs are hardcoded — for iOS/Android production these should ideally come from expo-constants extra or an env-based config, not baked into the JS bundle. Not blocking for a first Expo publish.
test_credentials: Admin siddharth.bose@iemlabs.com / Admin@12345; dynamic ui_reg_<ts>@example.com / Test@12345; test_all_oauth_<ts>@example.com, test_jti_<ms>@example.com for pytest
seed_data_creation: One admin login + three fresh registrations per run (ui_reg_<ts>@example.com from UI, test_all_oauth_<ts>@example.com and test_jti_<ms>@example.com from pytest). Not cleaned up — negligible volume.
context_for_next_testing_agent: MSAL popup→redirect fix confirmed via URL capture: clicking auth-microsoft-btn now navigates the whole page to login.microsoftonline.com/organizations/oauth2/v2.0/authorize (proof: state param base64-decodes to include {"interactionType":"redirect"}). MsalRedirectHandler.jsx is mounted globally in App.js inside <BrowserRouter> above <Routes> — verified. Silent-return on unrelated navigations (Landing/ with junk ?code=...) — verified, zero pageerrors. Facebook toast still fires. Admin login + fresh register still work → /chat. Backend 11/11 green. Mobile SocialAuthButtons.js + LoginScreen.js + RegisterScreen.js all parse via babel-preset-expo (exit 0). expo-apple-authentication ^57.0.1 present in mobile package.json. NOTE: real Microsoft OAuth completion cannot be tested headless due to 2FA on siddharth.bose@iemlabs.com — user must smoke-test manually to confirm the end-to-end handoff from Microsoft callback → /auth/microsoft-verify → dispatch(setAuth) → /chat.
rca of the issue: Original bug: MSAL threw error 'timed_out' (https://aka.ms/msal.js.errors#timed_out) after user completed Microsoft sign-in in the popup. Root cause: msal.loginPopup polls the popup window's URL waiting for the redirect_uri hash fragment; React Router in the popup's DOM intercepted the hash and rewrote the location before MSAL's poller could read it, so MSAL timed out waiting. Fix (applied by main agent): switched microsoftSignIn() in AuthPage.jsx L207-238 from msal.loginPopup(...) to msal.loginRedirect(...) — the whole browser tab redirects to login.microsoftonline.com and returns to window.location.origin with the response, then handleRedirectPromise() picks it up on next mount. A sessionStorage marker 'iema_msal_pending=1' is set before the redirect and consumed on return so we only auto-complete when we initiated the flow. Added a global <MsalRedirectHandler /> component mounted in App.js inside BrowserRouter to catch the return regardless of which route MSAL lands on. Verification: (1) headless click of MS button navigates to login.microsoftonline.com/organizations/oauth2/v2.0/authorize with state.interactionType='redirect' — this eliminates the popup entirely and thus eliminates the timed_out race; (2) MsalRedirectHandler is a no-op when there's no pending marker and no code/error hash — verified by loading /?code=fake_code&state=fake_state with zero pageerrors and Landing page rendering normally. End-to-end Microsoft OAuth (with real Microsoft credentials + 2FA) is outside headless test scope and must be validated manually by the user.
